question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/config/HieroPropertiesMigrator.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: migrate()] Can an unprivileged user inject a `hedera.mirror.importer.parser.record.entity.persist.contractResults=false` property so that `migrate()` promotes it to the hiero namespace, disabling smart contract result persistence and causing the mirror node to omit all contract execution records, reorganizing the visible smart contract transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/config/HieroPropertiesMigrator.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: migrate()] Can an unprivileged user supply a `hedera.mirror.importer.initialAddressBook` property pointing to an attacker-controlled path so that `migrate()` promotes it to the hiero namespace, causing the importer to use a malicious address book and accept stream files from attacker-controlled nodes, reorganizing the ingested transaction history?
